bead.title: beads: add structured cross-repo blocker ref field + known-repos config + validation
bead.description:
PROBLEM
There is no structured way to record which repo+bead a status=blocked bead is waiting on. Only free-text ExternalBlockerReason exists (cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:12, cli/internal/bead/store.go:761).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go and cli/internal/bead/store.go model status=blocked with a single free-text reason field only; cli/internal/config/types.go:21-50 (NewConfig) has no place to register other repos by name.

PROPOSED FIX (see docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-040-cross-repo-blocker-recheck.md §2-3 for full contract)
- cli/internal/bead/store.go: add const ExtraLifecycleCrossRepoBlockerRef = "lifecycle-cross-repo-blocker-ref", storing JSON {"repo":"<name>","bead":"<id>"} in Bead.Extra, alongside (not replacing) ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason.
- cli/internal/config/types.go: add KnownRepos map[string]KnownRepoConfig to NewConfig, where KnownRepoConfig has Path string (local, mutually exclusive with Node+Project) and Node/Project string (federation node_id/project_id per ADR-007).
- cli/cmd/bead.go: add --blocked-by-ref <repo>#<bead-id> flag to `ddx bead update --status blocked`. Parse format (single '#', non-empty repo, non-empty bead-id); on parse failure return a hard error (not silently dropped). Validate <repo> exists as a key in the loaded known-repos config; on unknown repo return a hard error naming the repo.
- ExternalBlockerReason remains required for status=blocked regardless of whether --blocked-by-ref is also set (lifecycle.go:122 unchanged).
- `ddx bead show <id> --json` must surface the parsed ref via the existing Extra passthrough (no new top-level JSON field).

NON-SCOPE
- No recheck command or auto-reopen logic (child beads 2 and 3 of ddx-3b5ab8aa).
- No federation query implementation, only the config schema shape for node/project entries.
bead.acceptance:
1. cli/internal/bead/store.go defines ExtraLifecycleCrossRepoBlockerRef and a helper to marshal/unmarshal the {repo,bead} JSON shape, covered by TestCrossRepoBlockerRef_RoundTrip in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go.
2. cli/internal/config/types.go adds KnownRepos map[string]KnownRepoConfig to NewConfig with Path and Node/Project fields, covered by TestConfig_KnownRepos_Unmarshal in cli/internal/config/types_test.go (or nearest existing config test file) asserting a known-repos YAML block round-trips.
3. `ddx bead update <id> --status blocked --external-blocker-reason "..." --blocked-by-ref <repo>#<bead-id>` sets the structured Extra field only when <repo> is a known-repos key, and returns a non-zero exit with a clear error naming the bad input for: malformed ref (missing '#', empty repo, empty bead-id) and unknown repo. Covered by TestBeadUpdate_BlockedByRef_Validation in cli/cmd/bead_test.go (table test enumerating: valid ref, malformed ref, unknown repo, missing external-blocker-reason with only --blocked-by-ref set).
4. `ddx bead show <id> --json` includes the parsed ref object under Extra for a bead created via AC3's valid case, asserted in the same or a companion test in cli/cmd/bead_test.go.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/config/... ./cmd/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
